video_title: The Thursday Murder Club | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text: @bedaineman That was my thought too. I'm not saying we shouldn't enjoy them both, we should. But it does very much feel like this is an attempt to a UK version of an Only Murders in the Building as opposed to bringing something that's very new to the table. It feels more like a good, safe investment, and I'm SURE that they're trying to market to the audience of and capitalize of the success on it's predecessor (in terms of media production). Regardless of when the books were published, this is Netflix competing with Hulu. It's just that simple. In fact, Netflix WANTS you to make that connection. They WANT you to watch this trailer and say, oh I loved Only Murders and this looks similar, so I'll probably like this too. The trailer is marketing and that's their goal, so if people are making the frankly blatant connection, I don't think it's exactly fair to criticize people for that. If there's anyone to blame for make an obvious comparison, it's Netflix's marketing department.

reply_text: These are good books written by an Englishman about an English retirement home and the people in it that happen to be pretty good at sleuthing.  I read the first book. I’m on the second. It’s usually pretty dry and sarcastic instead of slapstick or over the top. Only murders in the building brought that kind of sarcastic, wit, and humour with it as well, which is probably why it did so well. I can’t wait for this. If you’ve ever listened to an actor, describe which is harder doing a TV show or a movie they usually say a TV show.
